fresh_facts.claim: Operation Epic Fury, a 38-day US military campaign against Iran, resulted in destruction of Iran's ballistic missile and drone capabilities, navy, over 20 naval facilities, and nearly 80% of its nuclear industrial base according to Pentagon claims.
Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on the first day of the 40-day US-Iran conflict.

A three-member Provisional Leadership Council consisting of President Pezeshkian, judiciary chief Gholamhossein Mohseni-Ejei, and cleric Alireza Arafi was established to hold interim power following Khamenei's death.
The Israeli strike on the Assembly of Experts in Qom on March 3 delayed the process of selecting a new Supreme Leader.
A US-Iran ceasefire lasting two weeks was announced following Operation Epic Fury.
US-Iran negotiations are scheduled to take place in Islamabad, Pakistan amid the ceasefire.
President Trump warned Iran against charging tolls or blocking the Strait of Hormuz on April 9.
Turkish President Erdoğan discussed the US-Iran truce with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and urged lasting peace on April 9.
Pakistan proposed a bridging proposal that salvaged US-Iran talks before a US deadline on April 9.
War damage to Iran is estimated in the billions of euros, severely affecting infrastructure, military, and population.
Commercial tankers are avoiding the Strait of Hormuz despite the ceasefire due to ongoing threats.
Russian official Dmitry Medvedev claimed on April 9 that Iran has re-closed the Strait of Hormuz as a tactical advantage.
US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth claimed on April 8 that the US achieved a decisive military victory and functionally destroyed Iran's missile program.
Israel has paused operations in Iran but continues intensive strikes on Hezbollah.
Multiple Iranian leaders including spy chiefs, generals, and ministers were eliminated during the 40-day conflict.
A former Iranian foreign minister died from attack wounds on April 9.
Iran accepted the ceasefire under overwhelming pressure after US strikes spared some targets.
The Iranian regime has tightened control through executions and mass arrests to suppress unrest following the conflict.
Parliament speaker Mohammad Qalibaf, who is also IRGC commander, wields significant influence in post-war Iran's political structure.
The IRGC has supplanted religious authorities in Iran's power structure following the war.
President Trump declared the war against Iran a complete success on April 9.
NATO Secretary General Rutte called the alliance slow to aid US operations against Iran on April 9.
US Democrats pushed an Iran War Powers resolution that was blocked by Republicans on April 9.
The Center for Strategic and International Studies warned on April 8 that the ceasefire masks unresolved nuclear ambitions and terrorism risks.
Bitcoin rallied toward $73,000 on April 9 on ceasefire relief and war de-escalation.

regime_fall.current_status: Supreme Leader Khamenei was killed on March 1, triggering establishment of a Provisional Leadership Council (Pezeshkian, Mohseni-Ejei, Arafi) to hold interim power while a new Supreme Leader is selected. A two-week US-Iran ceasefire holds amid ongoing negotiations in Islamabad, but tensions escalate over Strait of Hormuz closure, with the IRGC consolidating power and hardliners like Parliament Speaker Qalibaf emerging as key power brokers.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed on March 1, 2026, during the opening phase of the 40-day US-Iran conflict, prompting formation of a Provisional Leadership Council and destabilizing Iran's succession structure.
